fresh_facts.claim: The US deployed a third aircraft carrier, USS George H. W. Bush, to the Middle East on March 7, 2026.
The US military struck more than 7,800 targets in Iran as part of a combined US-Israeli campaign.
Israeli airstrikes on March 17 targeted and killed senior Iranian officials Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani (Basij chief), confirmed by Iranian state media.
Israel launched a ground invasion of southern Lebanon on March 17, with Israeli Defense Forces continuing ground operations in the region.
Iranian missile strikes on March 17 in response to Israeli attacks killed two Israeli civilians in Ramat Gan.
On March 15, IRGC Aerospace Force Commander Brigadier General Majid Mousavi claimed Iran launched a Sejjil ballistic missile at Israel for the first time during the war.

US and Israeli strikes on March 14-15 targeted ballistic missile production facilities west of Tehran, the Malard Missile Launch Site, air force bases in Dezful and Bandar Abbas, and an IRGC Navy ammunition depot in Bushehr Province.
US and Israeli intelligence assess that approximately 200 air defense systems have been struck, leaving Iran with approximately 1,000 ballistic missiles remaining.
US and Israeli intelligence reportedly assess that the IRGC is making decisions because Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is wounded.
Kataib Hezbollah conducted two drone attacks against the former US Victory Base near Baghdad International Airport on March 14.

US and Israeli forces struck multiple Popular Mobilization Forces positions in Babylon and Kirkuk provinces on March 15, killing eight fighters of an Iranian-backed militia unit in al Qaim, Anbar Province.
Ali Khamenei, Iran's previous supreme leader, died on February 28, 2026, with his son Mojtaba Khamenei rapidly elevated as successor.

Iranian officials including Judiciary Chief Gholamhossein Mohseni Ejei, President Masoud Pezeshkian, and Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf publicly vowed continuity and revenge following Ali Larijani's death on March 17.
Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard testified to the Senate Intelligence Committee on March 18 that Iran's government 'appears to be intact, but largely degraded due to attacks.'
Strikes on Iran's South Pars gas complex occurred on March 18, with the IRGC confirming that gas tanks and refinery sections were hit.
In response to strikes on South Pars, Iran halted gas flows to Iraq to divert supply for domestic use.
Brent crude surged above $108 and toward $110 following strikes on Iran's energy infrastructure on March 18.
Saudi Arabia intercepted four ballistic missiles over Riyadh hours before an emergency consultative meeting of Arab and Islamic foreign ministers.
Israel claimed it killed Iranian Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib in an overnight strike on March 18.
Iranian missiles fired in retaliation for Ali Larijani's death killed two people near Tel Aviv, bringing Israel's death toll in the war to 14.
The Revolutionary Guards announced a new 'Wave 59' salvo of missiles and drones in response to Israeli strikes.
U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres called for the war in the Middle East to stop and urged full implementation of Security Council resolutions.
Turkey announced NATO was deploying another Patriot system to Adana to reinforce defenses against missile threats.
Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi asserted that Iran's government remained structurally strong despite individual losses and stated Iran's doctrine against nuclear weapons was unlikely to change.
The National Council of Resistance of Iran announced a detailed six-month plan for a transitional government in the event the current regime falls, pledging free elections within six months.
fresh_facts.source: National Council of Resistance of Iran

fresh_facts.claim: Unspecified advisers to President Trump told Axios on February 18 that Trump is frustrated with insufficient progress toward an agreement with Iran.
The IDF targeted Hezbollah positions including launch sites in Qatrani, Jezzine District, and Hezbollah Radwan Force headquarters in Beirut on March 14.

regime_fall.current_status: The Iranian regime faces an unprecedented convergence of existential pressures following Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death and Mojtaba Khamenei's succession, compounded by intensive coordinated US-Israeli military operations that have systematically degraded military command structures, strategic infrastructure, and economic capacity. While regime officials maintain public assertions of continuity and structural integrity, the convergence of wounded succession leadership, 7,800+ US military strikes, Israeli elimination of senior command including Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib and Basij chief Gholamreza Soleimani, economic infrastructure damage to South Pars gas complex, and persistent missile exchanges indicates critical vulnerabilities across all domains of regime stability.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US and Israeli forces conducted comprehensive strikes on March 17-18 targeting Iranian military command structures and economic infrastructure, killing senior officials including Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib and approximately 300+ Basij commanders, striking over 7,800 targets, damaging South Pars gas complex, and degrading air defense and ballistic missile capabilities, while Iran responded with ballistic missile salvos and announced 'Wave 59' retaliation.
